options: A single piece attacking two or more enemy pieces at once
A piece defended by another piece
Two pieces trading places on the board
questions.explanation: A fork is when a single piece attacks two or more enemy pieces simultaneously. Knights are the best forkers because their L-shape can attack squares of both colors.

questions.prompt: Find the square for the white knight on e5 to fork the black king on d8 and the black queen on f6.
questions.explanation: Nd7+! The knight lands on d7, attacking the king on d8 (check!) and the queen on f6. Black must respond to the check, and White wins the queen next move.

questions.prompt: Which piece is the most effective for forking in chess?
questions.explanation: Knights are the best forkers. Their L-shaped move can attack squares of any color. Bishops only attack one color. Rooks and queens attack in straight lines, which the opponent can usually block.
